text: Robotic ports are placed, and the surgeon assesses the peritoneum, revealing macronodular liver cirrhosis.

The surgeon uses suction to remove gastrointestinal spillage and fibrinous exudate. Gentle blunt dissection is performed in the hepato-duodenal area to inspect and identify the site of the duodenal ulcer perforation.
The surgeon identifies a perforation on the anterior wall of the duodenum, located near the duodenal bulb.

The surgeon uses a 3-0 silk suture to repair the duodenal ulcer perforation, placing full-thickness sutures approximately 0.5 centimeters from the friable edges, extending from one margin to the other. The suture now includes the posterior duodenal wall.
The surgeon mobilizes a patch of viable omentum and positions it over the duodenal perforation without tension. Successive sutures are tied to securely anchor the omental patch in place.
The surgeon irrigates the peritoneal cavity with a large volume of warm saline, focusing on cleaning the suprahepatic and subhepatic recesses and pelvis to remove any remaining contamination.
